name: pruvalex-pruvagraph
description: The unified engine for cost-optimized and secure LLM interactions.

pruvagraph.dedupThreshold.description: Jaccard similarity threshold for semantic dedup (0.5–1.0). Lower = more aggressive grouping.

enumDescriptions: Free — code only (tree-sitter + regex). No API key needed.
Claude Haiku for docs/PDFs. Needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
Gemini Flash for docs/PDFs. Needs GEMINI_API_KEY.
Local Ollama — free, needs Ollama running.
pruvagraph.llmBackend.description: LLM backend for doc/PDF extraction (code files are always free).

pruvagraph.maxFilesPerBatch.description: Max files to process in one batch (for progress reporting).

pruvagraph.monorepoMode.description: [M1] Auto-detect monorepo layout and build per-package graphs on every build.

pruvagraph.modules.driftguard.enabled.description: DriftGuard: validates AI-suggested imports against your installed packages on every save. Renders invalid imports as VS Code diagnostics.
pruvagraph.modules.driftguard.enabled.markdownDescription: **DriftGuard** validates AI-suggested code against your actual installed dependency versions on every file save. Catches hallucinated or wrong-version API calls and renders them as native VS Code diagnostics (warning severity). Disable to skip on-save import validation.

pruvagraph.modules.contextlens.enabled.description: ContextLens: logs every MCP tool call with token cost. MCP tools: get_active_context, measure_token_usage, trace_last_tool_calls.
pruvagraph.modules.contextlens.enabled.markdownDescription: **ContextLens** gives you visibility into what PruvaGraph has injected into your AI's context this session. Every MCP tool call is automatically logged with its token cost estimate. Three MCP tools: `get_active_context` (session summary + per-tool breakdown), `measure_token_usage` (total token cost), `trace_last_tool_calls` (recent call history). Disable to turn off session logging.

pruvagraph.modules.ghostmemory.enabled.description: GhostMemory: persists decisions, tasks, and blockers across Claude Code sessions via remember/recall MCP tools.
pruvagraph.modules.ghostmemory.enabled.markdownDescription: **GhostMemory** persists decisions, tasks, and blockers across Claude Code sessions via `remember`/`recall` MCP tools and auto-injects them into CLAUDE.md. Disable to stop session memory persistence.

pruvagraph.modules.rulesforge.enabled.description: RulesForge: detects file layer (api/ui/test/util/config) via AST and returns context-appropriate AI coding rules. MCP tools: get_applicable_rules, learn_from_accept.
pruvagraph.modules.rulesforge.enabled.markdownDescription: **RulesForge** detects your current file's architectural layer (api/ui/test/util/config) via AST analysis and returns context-appropriate AI coding rules. Unlike static `.cursorrules` files, these rules change based on what file the agent is working on. Two MCP tools: `get_applicable_rules` (layer detection + rules) and `learn_from_accept` (capture patterns from accepted AI suggestions). Disable to skip context-aware rule injection.

pruvagraph.modules.taskweaver.enabled.description: TaskWeaver: checkpoints agent runs as a SQLite DAG with git micro-commits. MCP tools: create_checkpoint, get_task_progress, rollback_to_checkpoint, list_checkpoints.
pruvagraph.modules.taskweaver.enabled.markdownDescription: **TaskWeaver** checkpoints multi-step agent runs as a SQLite DAG with real git micro-commits, so an interrupted session can resume from the last good state without re-paying for context. Four MCP tools: `create_checkpoint`, `get_task_progress`, `rollback_to_checkpoint`, `list_checkpoints`. Rollback is advisory — surfaces the git command but never executes destructive operations without explicit consent. Disable to stop checkpoint creation.

pruvagraph.modules.budgetgovernor.enable.description: BudgetGovernor: tracks token spend per session and enforces caps. MCP dispatch auto-estimates tokens for every tool call.
pruvagraph.modules.budgetgovernor.enable.markdownDescription: **BudgetGovernor** tracks token spend per session and enforces a configurable token cap. Every MCP tool call flows through `_dispatch()` which estimates tokens as `len(result) // 4` and records spend automatically. Thresholds: OK (<80%), WARNING (80–99%), EXCEEDED (≥100%). Disable to stop token budget tracking.

scripts.coverage: nyc --reporter=text --reporter=html --reporter=lcov node test/test_extension_driftguard.js && nyc --reporter=text --reporter=html --reporter=lcov --no-clean node test/test_dashboard_html.js && nyc report
scripts.lint: node --check extension.js src/utils.js src/cli-runner.js src/commands.js src/driftguard.js src/sidebar-provider.js src/sidebar-html.js src/dashboard.js src/telemetry.js
